title: Messaging Outcomes and Delivery Evidence in Go · Runtime Contract: Ownership Handoff Map
summary: A Go 1.26 service team must separate protocol custody from application effects with explicit structs and table-driven outcomes; reviewers reject hidden goroutine ownership, unbounded work, stale channel state, and claims that stop at a nil client error. The learner predicts values, ownership transitions, and stop paths before seeing a model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the ownership handoff map increment in a typed outcome and ownership evidence record. Learners progress through prediction…
objectives: Map message ownership from producer intent through local buffer, broker confirmation, queue, delivery, consumer acknowledgement, and durable effect; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.

State at-most-once, at-least-once, effectively-once effect, ordering, and durability claims without promising impossible end-to-end exactly-once delivery; in Go, retain ownership, stop-path, and changed-case evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and Go Predict Ownership Handoff Map · Theory Ownership Handoff Map
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Go messaging team handles case 8186 during confirm-window load test with broker flow control. Build deterministic Go evidence to separate local publication, broker custody, and observable stakeholder effect; name the owner and stop path for every goroutine and notification channel; then fail the canary and prove older Go consumers remain compatible. Browser code uses pure values and state transitions only. amqp091-go, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, race and leak checks, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Ownership Handoff Map contract for the supplied data.
